blogs.title: recent favorite/underated machines in the gym you should try if you haven't already
blogs.article: my physique goals recently is to have a runner's build, lean and cut, improved cardio, and as a bonus, dunking ability and abs/v-taper. as for muscle, i am not trying to get big/ put on a lot of muscle while lifting heavy like i did in college. i am leaning more towards body weight exercises, focusing on number of reps more than the weight. recently some of the machines i enjoy are, number one: the seated leg press - this machine allows you to walk faster on the streets and gives you more stability when you run or walk in everyday motion. i remember when i didnt leave the house in over 3 months and havent used the gym in more than 6 months, the first time i went outside i felt so slow, i could not walk properly, i felt like i would be off balance and fall. i couldn't activate my leg muscles and could not flex any muscle independently and was mostly going through the motion of how i remembered walking to be. in order to run long distances as you need to as a runner, you need to have proper running, jogging and walking form and if you don't, repeating improper form for long periods of time will cause an injury or imbalance in the impact causing excessive load/pain in untargeted areas as opposed to working out the right muscle and feeling the pain there to stengthen it. with improper form, i would feel pain in my shins, hips, my liver, knees, ankles when i run, but with proper form it is all in my quads and a little bit of hamstrings and core. number two: standing ab crunch, this machine is unique to the lifefitness machines and i like it because it is a good starter machine to develop the core as opposed to situps if your core is not strong to begin with because if you do situps without activating your core or using improper form, you will strain your lower back. how i would use these machines is i would start with a low weight but still be able to feel a burn when i go past twenty reps, the first 20 reps is to manually feel out the muscle and isolate it on the reps, and the reps after 20 is to hold the form and stress out the muscle until you feel a burn and cant go further. sometimes this is over 100 reps. that is when i will take a break, when i cannot go another rep and increase my weight slightly usually by 5 or 10 lbs. i will repeat this until i cannot lift a weight that is heavier, i do this for both machines. during the upper half of my college years, my favorite machines were the roman chair, bench press, and reverse preacher curls. i start off my workout on the romain chair to hang on it and stretch out my back before the bench press. i may also use a stretch band to stretch out my back as well as biceps. i would then go do some dips to activate my shoulders because i can isolate and workout my shoulders, shoulder width apart to protect them from overstraining on the bench and providing good shoulder support for the bench when i lift some heavier weights and they overload my shoulder instead of using my back and chest like it should. i would also try to do some pull ups to workout my back, shoulders and biceps, doing a pull movement which will help on the bench when i counter the movement with push movements, making the bench press workout more thorough and comprehensive. it is kind of like stretching the muscle before working it. if i dont do a pull movement, i would feel my bicep is very tight, early in the bench press workout, one dimensional and stagnated. Number 2 - the bench press: this is my second machine and I focus on building up to higher weights, doing many reps at low weight to warm up the muscle first. in college i was focused on really getting my bench to as high as possible, i liked lifting heavy and was like fk it, im gonna try to get as massive as possible. it was cool when your whole life was in college, not really going out much and alternating between dorm, gym and library but when i left college, i could not wear any tight/ slimfit comfort shirts because everything would be showing and i had to usually wear oversized, dense, drop shoulder covering shirts that would get really hot and uncomfortable with no free range of motion. Number 3 is the reverse preacher curls - i like to do this with the angled bar and not the straight bar as it really activates my forearms and gets it really lean. also in the bench press you use a lot of forearm strength to stabilize the bar as it comes down thats why working out the forearms helps improve your bench press weight and form. Number 4 is the reverse flys - this workout is really good at stretching my back and activating my back, i enjoy this workout so much. sometimes when i sleep for long periods or sleep on a soft mattress, my back feels so tight and imbalanced but when i do the reverse flys, i feel like i am aligning my spine correctly and cracking my back in the process. i always start with very low weight, something i can do 50 plus with and work it like i am stretching. then i will slowly increase the weight to strengthen it but i am more focused/ isolating the muscle to strengthen it. Number 5 is the seated ab machine - i really liked this machine and started using it in 404 and then at nyu sh. similarly, start at low weight to isolate the abs and aim for as high reps as possible, to target the correct muscle and prevent straining the lower back. In the lower half of my college years the machines i enjoyed were number one has to be the inclined chest press. i really liked that i could feel the weight on my back and shoulders and i worked out those parts to strengthen it before going to the flat bench. i rarely liked sitting on the flat bench, i didnt like laying down but inclined bench has a really comfortable seat and i would spend a long time on that machine. this helped devlop shoulders and back as well as upper chest. Number 2 is the inclined shoulder press with dumbels. doing this workout while facing the mirror, you seem massive as you can see your biceps activated from stabilizing the dumbells, something you dont get from the machine. this workout definitely has a performative effect to it but it really helps with shoulder and back. if not as performative i would use the machine instead as all the equipment is covering up everything but if is was only me in the gym or the gym is pretty empty and everyone is spaced out, this is the workout i would do. Number 3 is overhead press - this workout helps with developing shoulders which pairs with the inclined bench workout. it also was cool to lift something over your head and helped to work out back and upper chest in the meantime to stabilize the weight. Number 4 would be the tricep pull downs - i was just starting out in the gym and working my triceps was something i felt was a really good stretch and that machine made me really feel like a gym goer, you can really feel the tricep tighten up, something you dont get in normal day to day activites. it also makes doing diamond pushups a lot easier, something that seemed hard in elementary/middle school. Number 5 is dips on the roman chair again - it helps with uses core and shoulders to stabilize and works out the shoulders and triceps, strengthening them for safer workout on the bench. End of highschool was the first time i really went to the gym - more so sophmore year was when i really began lifting weights. i was afraid lifting weights early would stunt my growth but in basketball practice, our coach brought us to lift weights and i first was introduced to machines in the gym through that experience which shaped my identity as a gym goer from a basketball player's standpoint. Number one - the leg press. this machine i was so suprised with because i enjoyed working out my legs and my upper body was not strong at the time but i was suprised by how strong my legs are and by using the leg press, i could hit weights that gave me a ego boost.

blogs.title: food products I love to eat for fitness, health and getting in shape
blogs.article: i love sugary drinks but that has gotten in the way of my fitness goals of getting lean and cut. premade drinks like gatorade and other bottled drinks that i love at the asian grocery store is full of sugar and artificial ingredients. asian drinks are better because the ingredients are safe to consume however american drinks have ingredients that straight up cause cancer. i have been re-evaluating american products lately like cheese that doesnt melt and burn like plastic, juices that contain less than 10% juice, which motivated me to start listing my favorite products and be more consious of the brands I am consuming instead of shopping whereever is convenient. first product is organic coconut water - specifically the pink coconut water which they have in kirkland (costco), trader joes and harmless harvest. i found harmless harvest to be a bit too sweet and slightly artificially sweet compared to the kirkland and trader joes ones which are refreshing and light but nonetheless serve as good options when you want something sweet to drink instead of drinking water all the time. Product 2 is freshly squeezed orange juice from whole foods or buy your own electronic juicer - i love to drink orange juice for something sweet and citrusy but have been drinking tropicana for a long time, it does not taste anything close to freshly squeezed orange juice. it is sour and builds up flem in your mouth while having a bunch of ingredients in it that is not just orange juice which you would want. after drinking freshly squeezed orange juice from whole foods, i have considered buying a electronic juicer and oranges instead. Product 3 - sous vide packaged chicken breast. chicken breast i found is one of the things that has given me lots of energy and not feel bloated after eating since i am sitting a lot and help me lean down. after eating white meat chicken, i feel energized, full, and do not feel lethargic like carbs and fatty foods do. steak is another option but it doesnt get me full as well as fish. cooking chicken breast is also something i had to learn to avoid it being dry. i used to cook a pack of chicken breast in college which was two breasts and by pan frying right out of the package, the juices would run out and the chicken became dry and stringy. i thought this was how chicken was supposed to taste and i had to just put up with it to get the benefits but recently i found out dry brining or marinating has made chicken juicier and allowed it to keep its moisture while cooking which made it more enjoyable to eat and achieve my fitness goals. another method that allows chicken to lock in its juices is sous videing and having it packaged is very convenient to store and meal prep. Product 4 - yogurt (avocado flavored): yogurt is another sweet snack i like and yogurt is great for digestion. i like yogurt, its easy to eat, has fruit flavored ones which taste great. i also really like the drinkable yogurts. in china there was a popular yogurt drink brand called more yogurt which is most famous for their avocado yogurt smoothie and that flavor is healthy, rich and fragrant which i think makes a really great yogurt flavor. Product 5 - sour plum drink: this drink i had some getting used to, initially i found it to be really sour and almost medicinal tasting but recently i found that this drink is high in antioxidants, good for degreasing and cutting through oils and the sourness wakes up the taste buds as well as the mind.

blogs.title: the vision for the brand, get active foods
blogs.article: the vision for the brand, get active foods is a brand that promotes eating foods that make you healthier, more active, more energetic in the gym, less sluggish, less sleepy, more fit and that's all. just like any brand/digital platform, it influences you to act a certain way, this platform makes it easy to get the foods you need for your active life. inspired by impeccable chicken as well as packaged chicken in china, i wondered why someone would trust eating cooked chicken from a package. but i realized it is optimized on all vectors. protein that you can eat until you are full that wont make you sleepy, have energy, as your stomache is burning protein all day long making you fit and awake, cooked sous vide giving it the perfect juicy tender cook which can be dry if you dont know how to cook chicken yourself (secret i learned after eating dry stringy throughout college it to brine your chicken overnight, can be as simple as salting it to marinating), and the most convenient product - pre packaged easy to eat like opening a bag of chips or a protein bar. these three pillars - keeps you energetic, active and awake (no carbs) - get you fit - and is convenient is the measure for all products in get active foods. and part of getting fit, energetic, active and healthy is organic, natural, simple ingredients, no added ingredients, no mysterious ingredients. growing up not really thinking twice and buying brands that was 'convenient' ly placed in the grocery store is why we consumed orange juice that tastes more sour than sweet and has more added sugars than soda in order for it to taste like juice from an orange. being hooked on that taste of orange juice and after drinking freshly squeezed orange juice, i then realized how artificially, slightly bitter (wondering if the taste comes from blending in the rinds as well), very sour and high fructose corn syrupey (always left you with orange-yellow flem on your tongue after drinking a sip) tropicana orange juice tasted like because it was just that, 'convenient' ly placed and positioned - influencing and developing the habit of what we think orange juice should taste like without thinking twice. it wasn't until after college that i thought of buying a juice press and oranges instead. pointing out the 'that's just the way it tastes product' to 'that's not the way it should taste' and offering how 'it should taste' by simply offering it on its own. the brands managed to do so much to it only to make it taste weirder and more unusual from it's natural state. Sure you can make all these foods and drinks at home as long as you're consuming natural foods with minmal ingredients, drinks w no added sugars, chicken sous vide with just salt, dried beef with just salt, recommend products and share recipes.
